content:
Work Hard, Think High, Touch the Sky...

To every young, budding lawyer in India: your hard work today is building the advocate you will be tomorrow.

The truth is simple—every great lawyer you admire started as a junior, reading files late into the night and learning from every loss. 

You don’t need a famous surname or a foreign degree; you need consistency, humility, and the courage to stay in the game.

Show up to court even when you have no matter listed. Observe arguments, watch how seniors respond to the Bench, and take notes like a student of the craft. Invest in your language skills, your research abilities, and your ethics—these are your real assets.[superlawyer]

Say yes to challenging work, including pro bono matters, because they sharpen your instincts and remind you why the law matters. 

Build relationships, not just contacts; mentors, colleagues, clerks, and clients will shape your journey.

There will be days of doubt, lean months, and tough hearings—but if you keep learning, keep showing up, and keep your integrity intact, you will build a meaningful practice.

If you’re a young lawyer reading this, keep going. The profession needs your energy, your ideas, and your voice.

Best Wishes 

S K Singhi 
Founder & Managing Partner
S K Singhi & Partners LLP
comment: One skill that often gets overlooked early on is judgment—knowing which points not to argue. Research and preparation matter, but restraint can be just as valuable in court.
